etymology.description: semantic 彳(彳) (chì, "walk") + phonetic 复(复) (fù, "repeat") -> walk back and forth
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the element 彳(彳) (chì, "walk"), suggesting movement or return, with the phonetic element 复(复) (fù, "repeat"), giving the sense of walking repeatedly in the same path, which embodies the idea of returning or doing something again.
etymology.simplification: The character 復(復) was simplified to 复 by retaining the phonetic component 复(复) and dropping the semantic 彳(彳) (chì, "walk").

examples.chinese: 他希望能复读一年(他希望能復讀一年)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xīwàng néng fùdú yī nián.
examples.english: He hopes to repeat a year in school.

examples.chinese: 老师要我们复习上节课的内容(老師要我們複習上節課的內容)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī yào wǒmen fùxí shàng jié kè de nèiróng.
examples.english: The teacher wants us to review the content from the last class.

examples.chinese: 每次复活节(每次複活節)，全家人都会团聚(全家人都會團聚)。
examples.pinyin: Měi cì fùhuó jié, quánjiā rén dōu huì tuánjù.
examples.english: Every Easter, the whole family will reunite.
